---
title: "Module 1: Citation Management"
subtitle: "Organize your references effectively"
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
#### 🎯 Learning Objectives

After completing this module, you will be able to:

- Set up and use a citation management tool (Zotero or Paperpile)
- Organize and sync your reference library
- Generate citations and bibliographies in any citation style
- Understand what a Biosketch is and how to create one for grant applications
:::

---

## Overview

This module introduces citation management and two tools: **Zotero** and **Paperpile**. It also covers the **Biosketch**, a document required for federal grant applications.

> ✅ Complete the assignment at the end of this module and the associated knowledge check quiz on Canvas to earn credit toward your badge.

---

## Why Citation Management Matters

Manually tracking hundreds of references is error-prone and time-consuming. Citation management software lets you:

- Capture references from databases, websites, and PDFs in one click
- Auto-generate citations and bibliographies in any style (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.)
- Sync your library across devices and collaborate with team members
- Attach and annotate PDFs alongside your references

See the [UCR Library Citation Management Guide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/referencemanagement) for an overview of all tools available to UCR students.

---

## Zotero vs. Paperpile

### Choosing the Right Tool

Both tools cover the same core workflow. Your choice mainly depends on where you write. Review the slides below for an overview, then read on for details on each tool.

| Feature | Zotero | Paperpile |
|---|---|---|
| Price | Free ([300 MB storage online](https://www.zotero.org/storage)) | Free with UCR email |
| Best for | Word, LibreOffice, [Google Docs](https://www.zotero.org/support/google_docs) | Google Docs, [Microsoft Word](https://paperpile.com/h/get-started-word/) |
| Browser extension | ✅ All browsers | ✅ Google Chrome |
| Collaboration | ✅ Group libraries | ✅ Shared folders |
| Mobile app | ✅ | ✅ |
| PDF annotation | ✅ Built-in | ✅ Built-in |

```{=html}
<iframe src="https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1RHQclRtv3HbgBarlz9zVlBQUYoh456Im/embed?start=false&loop=false&delayms=3000"
  width="100%" height="480" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
```

### Zotero

[Zotero](https://www.zotero.org/) is free and open source, works with Microsoft Word, LibreOffice, and Google Docs, and lets you sync your library across devices.

**Getting started:**

1. Download Zotero at [zotero.org](https://www.zotero.org/)
2. Install the **[Zotero Connector](https://www.zotero.org/download/connectors)** browser extension to save references in one click
3. Create a free Zotero account to sync across devices
4. See the [UCR Citation Management Guide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/referencemanagement) for style-specific tips

Watch the workshop recording below for a full walkthrough:

```{=html}
<iframe width="100%" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/Fq6N-KzxvpY?list=PLaDWxNwvAu5bQrpeV7ORCiw6OY1a215Hm" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>
```

[▶ View full Citation Management playlist on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLaDWxNwvAu5bQrpeV7ORCiw6OY1a215Hm)

### Paperpile

[Paperpile](https://paperpile.com/) is available free to UCR students using your UCR email address. It lives entirely inside Google Docs — no separate desktop app needed.

**Getting started:**

1. Sign up at [paperpile.com](https://paperpile.com/) using your UCR email
2. Install the **Paperpile browser extension** (Chrome) and the **Google Docs add-on**
3. Import references directly from Google Scholar, PubMed, or by uploading PDFs

Paperpile now also supports **Microsoft Word** — install the [Paperpile Word add-in](https://paperpile.com/h/get-started-word/) if Word is your preferred writing environment.

See the [UCR Paperpile LibGuide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/paperpile) for step-by-step instructions and tips.

```{=html}
<iframe width="100%" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/odYFJRKqEdM?list=PLaDWxNwvAu5bQrpeV7ORCiw6OY1a215Hm" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>
```

[▶ View full Citation Management playlist on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLaDWxNwvAu5bQrpeV7ORCiw6OY1a215Hm)

---

## Citing Sources

Once your library is set up, both tools let you insert citations and auto-generate a bibliography in any style. For style-specific guidance (APA, MLA, Chicago, and more), see the [UCR How to Cite Sources Guide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/citing).

---

## Biosketches for Grant Applications

A **biosketch** is a short professional biography required by federal funders (NIH, NSF) as part of grant applications. It summarizes your education, positions, contributions to science, and research support.

- NIH and NSF each have slightly different biosketch formats
- The [UCR Library Biosketch Guide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/c.php?g=962000&p=7732817) walks you through each section step by step
- Creating your biosketch early — before grant deadlines — saves significant time

---

## Assignment

::: {.callout-important}
### 📋 Assignment: Screenshots of Your Citation Management Tool

Set up a citation management tool (Zotero or Paperpile) and submit screenshots showing:

1. Your citation library with at least **3 references** imported
2. Evidence of browser extension installation OR a citation exported to a document

**Submission:** Upload your screenshots to the Canvas course platform.

*This assignment is required for the badge.*
:::

---

## Get Help

- 📚 [UCR Library Citation Management Guide](https://guides.lib.ucr.edu/referencemanagement)
- 🗓️ UCR Library offers **synchronous Zotero workshops** — check [library.ucr.edu](https://library.ucr.edu) for upcoming sessions
- 📧 **STEM Teaching Librarian:** Mary-Michelle Moore · [marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu](mailto:marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu)
- 📧 **LibSTEM Team:** [libstemteam@ucr.edu](mailto:libstemteam@ucr.edu)

---

```{=html}
<div class="next-module-banner">
  <a href="02-scholarly-communication.qmd">
    <div>
      <div class="next-label">Up Next · Module 2</div>
      <div class="next-title">Scholarly Communication</div>
    </div>
    <div class="next-arrow">→</div>
  </a>
</div>
```
